reason: All pass criteria met. Playtest: PASS with no P0/P1 defects. Enrichment: 8 suggestions, none identifying blocking defects. Average 3.67, minimum 3. No red flags.

P2-2: Set difference appears in only L04-L05, then vanishes (mitigated: L05 reduces diff to intersection+complement)
P2-3: change+push_neg ceremony adds mechanical friction on complement hypotheses (Lean limitation, no authoring fix)
enrichmentSuggestionsToImplement: S4: Name the subset-equality gap in L09 conclusion — 'To prove S = ∅, prove S has no elements' (low effort, high reuse value)
S6: Name the change+push_neg pattern in L11 conclusion — give this recurring workflow a memorable label (low effort)
S1: Derive one De Morgan law from the other via double complement — new level showing results generate each other (high effort, highest mathematical impact)
S2: Absorption law s ∩ (s ∪ t) = s — new level covering a structural identity the world currently omits (high effort)
notes: World passes cleanly. The playtest found no P0/P1 defects and scored 3+ in all categories. The enrichment review's top suggestion (De Morgan derivation from double complement) is the single highest-value improvement but is an enhancement, not a defect — the world teaches both De Morgan laws correctly, it just doesn't show they are related. The two low-effort enrichment suggestions (S4, S6) are worth implementing if a fix round occurs for other reasons. The P2 defects are all documented design decisions or Lean limitations, none escalatable.
